prompt: Is voting compulsory in the Brazilian general election 2026 (Brazil)? What happens if I am eligible but do not vote?
answer_text:
Yes. In Brazil’s 2026 general election, voting is **compulsory for eligible voters for whom voting is mandatory**. The TSE’s 2026 election materials and voter guidance confirm that compulsory voting remains in force for the 2026 election. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes-2026?utm_source=openai))

That said, voting is **not mandatory for everyone**. It is generally **optional** for:
- people aged **16–17**,
- people aged **70 or older**, and
- people who are **illiterate**.  
The TSE also notes exceptions where cancellation for non-voting does not apply, such as when voting is optional or when a disability makes voting impossible or excessively burdensome. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/eleitor/titulo-e-local-de-votacao/voto-obrigatoriedade?utm_source=openai))

If you are **required to vote** and you **do not vote**, you should either:
1. **justify your absence**, or  
2. **pay the electoral fine**.  
If you miss **both rounds**, each round counts separately, so you must justify each one separately. The TSE says a voter may justify as many times as necessary. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/servicos-eleitorais/justificativa-eleitoral?utm_source=openai))

If you **do not vote, do not justify, and do not pay the fine**, you can become **not in good standing with the Electoral Justice system** and face restrictions. TSE guidance lists consequences including difficulty obtaining an electoral clearance certificate and restrictions on acts such as enrolling in or renewing enrollment at public educational institutions, taking office in public service, obtaining certain public documents, and other acts that require electoral regularity. The TSE also notes that one older lending-related restriction was **revoked by Law 14.960/2023**. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/eleitor/titulo-e-local-de-votacao/voto-obrigatoriedade?utm_source=openai))

A more serious consequence applies if you miss **three consecutive elections** without justification or payment of the fines: your **voter registration may be cancelled**. For this rule, **each round counts as one election**. So, for example, first round + second round + the next missed round can already total three consecutive elections. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/servicos-eleitorais/justificativa-eleitoral?utm_source=openai))

On deadlines, the TSE says a justification can generally be filed after the election, and for voters registered in Brazil who are abroad on election day, the deadline is **30 days after returning to Brazil**. The TSE’s 2026 justification page also states that entries related to unregistered justification requests for the 2026 first and second rounds must be recorded by **December 9, 2026**, which is an administrative date in the 2026 cycle. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/legislacao/compilada/res/2026/resolucao-no-23-759-de-26-de-fevereiro-de-2026?utm_source=openai))

If you want, I can also give you a **simple checklist** for your case:
- **living in Brazil**,  
- **outside your voting district on election day**, or  
- **living abroad**.

prompt: I am a citizen of Brazil living abroad. Can I vote in the Brazilian general election 2026, and if so what offices can I vote for?
answer_text:
Yes — **if your electoral registration is abroad** (that is, your *domicílio eleitoral* is in the **Zona Eleitoral do Exterior** and your status is regular), you **can vote in Brazil’s 2026 general election**, but **only for President and Vice President of the Republic**. Brazilians voting from abroad do **not** vote abroad for governor, senator, federal deputy, state/district deputy, or other state-level offices. ([international.tse.jus.br](https://international.tse.jus.br/en/elections/voters-abroad?utm_source=openai))

For **2026**, the TSE says the general election’s **first round is October 4, 2026**, and the **second round, if needed, is October 25, 2026**. The TSE also stated that Brazilians abroad needed to request issuance/transfer/regularization in time to be eligible, with the key deadline falling on **May 6, 2026** for this election cycle. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/legislacao/compilada/res/2026/resolucao-no-23-751-de-26-de-fevereiro-de-2026?utm_source=openai))

Important distinction: if you **live abroad but kept your electoral domicile in a municipality in Brazil**, then you are still tied to that Brazilian voting district. In that case, you are **not an overseas voter for abroad polling**, and you would remain responsible for the elections corresponding to that Brazilian municipality; if you are outside Brazil on election day, you generally need to **justify your absence** rather than vote abroad. ([international.tse.jus.br](https://international.tse.jus.br/en/elections/voters-abroad?utm_source=openai))

So the practical answer is:

- **Yes, you can vote abroad in 2026** if your voter registration is properly registered **abroad** and regular. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/servicos-eleitorais/eleitorado-no-exterior?utm_source=openai))
- **The only offices you can vote for abroad are President and Vice President.** ([international.tse.jus.br](https://international.tse.jus.br/en/elections/voters-abroad?utm_source=openai))
- If your registration is still linked to a city in Brazil, you generally **cannot vote abroad for all offices**; instead, you would need to vote in Brazil or justify absence. ([international.tse.jus.br](https://international.tse.jus.br/en/elections/voters-abroad?utm_source=openai))

If you want, I can also help you determine **which of these applies to you** based on your current voter registration status.

prompt: Is voting compulsory in the Brazilian general election 2026 (Brazil)? What happens if I am eligible but do not vote?
answer_text:
Short answer: Yes — under Brazilian law the vote is compulsory for most adult citizens in the 2026 general election. If you are eligible (i.e., in the group for whom voting is mandatory) and do not vote you will be placed in debt with the Justiça Eleitoral and face administrative penalties until you regularize your situation (justify your absence or pay the fine). (Information below is current as of May 29, 2026.) ([wipo.int](https://www.wipo.int/wipolex/ar/legislation/details/15376?utm_source=openai))

Details

- Who must / may vote
  - Voting and electoral registration are obligatory for Brazilians 18–70 years old. Voting is facultative (optional) for those aged 16–17, people aged 70 or over, and for illiterate persons. ([wipo.int](https://www.wipo.int/wipolex/ar/legislation/details/15376?utm_source=openai))

- What happens if you don’t vote
  - You go into “débito” (irregularity) with the Electoral Justice; you cannot obtain a certidão de quitação eleitoral while the debt exists. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/eleitor/titulo-de-eleitor/justificativa-eleitoral?utm_source=openai))
  - While the debt is not regularized you may face temporary restrictions such as inability to: obtain or renew a passport or national identity document, enroll in public higher-education institutions, take part in public concursos (public‑sector entrance exams) or be appointed/take office in public functions, etc. (there are some statutory exceptions for Brazilians abroad needing to return). ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/comunicacao/noticias/2024/Julho/veja-as-consequencias-para-o-eleitor-que-nao-votou-nao-justificou-nem-pagou-multa?utm_source=openai))
  - If you repeatedly fail to vote and do not justify or pay fines for three consecutive elections, your voter registration may be cancelled; you would then need to re‑register to regain regular status. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/eleitor/titulo-de-eleitor/justificativa-eleitoral?utm_source=openai))

- Fines and regularization
  - You may avoid the fine by presenting a valid justification. If you miss doing so you will be charged a (judicial/administrative) fine; the calculation and exact value follow the Electoral Code and TSE rules (historically small and often handled via a GRU/boleto, PIX or card). The TSE and the regional TREs provide online services (e‑Título / Sistema Justifica / Autoatendimento) to justify absences and to consult/emit the payment (GRU) to clear any fines. Pay attention to the deadlines below. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/servicos-eleitorais/titulo-eleitoral-servicos/quitacao-de-multas?utm_source=openai))
  - Deadlines:
    - You can justify your absence on election day (at the polling place) or later by using e‑Título / Sistema Justifica or by submitting the Requerimento de Justificativa Eleitoral within 60 days after each turn (special rules apply if you were abroad: typically 30 days after return to Brazil to present proof). ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/servicos-eleitorais/justificativa-eleitoral?utm_source=openai))

What you should do (practical)
- If you missed voting in 2026 (or will miss it): within 60 days after the relevant turn, either (a) submit a justification via the e‑Título / Sistema Justifica or by sending the RJE to your zona eleitoral, or (b) if you cannot justify, consult your debits on the TSE/TRE portal and pay the fine (GRU / PIX / card). After payment or accepted justification you can issue the certidão de quitação eleitoral. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/servicos-eleitorais/justificativa-eleitoral?utm_source=openai))

If you want, I can:
- check the exact fine amount that would apply to your specific case (it can vary by rule/period) — tell me your state or whether you are currently abroad; or
- show the direct TSE pages and step‑by‑step links for justifying or paying via e‑Título.

prompt: I am a citizen of Brazil living abroad. Can I vote in the Brazilian general election 2026, and if so what offices can I vote for?
answer_text:
Short answer: Yes — if you are a Brazilian citizen living abroad you can vote in the 2026 general election, but only for President and Vice‑President (you cannot vote from abroad for governor, senator, federal/state/district deputies, mayor or municipal offices). ([mpce.mp.br](https://www.mpce.mp.br/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/RESOLUCAO-No-23.669-DE-14-DE-DEZEMBRO-DE-2021.-%E2%80%94-Tribunal-Superior-Eleitoral.pdf))

What you must do (essentials)
- Be registered as an elector resident abroad (inscribed in the Zona Eleitoral do Exterior — “Zona ZZ”). The Justice Electoral (TRE‑DF/TSE) only allows voting abroad for people who have their registration transferred/created in the Zona ZZ. ([temasselecionados.tse.jus.br](https://temasselecionados.tse.jus.br/temas-selecionados/eleitor-do-alistamento-ao-voto/votacao/voto-no-exterior))  
- Use the Título Net Exterior (Autoatendimento Eleitoral) or the nearest Brazilian consulate/embassy to request the first title, transfer your domicílio eleitoral to the exterior, or to update/regularize your registration. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/institucional/corregedoria-geral-eleitoral/sistemas-e-servicos-1/titulo-net-exterior?utm_source=openai))  
- Meet the registration deadline for the 2026 election: the TSE calendar set the deadline to request/transfer/revise the title for the 2026 pleito at 6 May 2026 (151 days before the first round on 4 October 2026). If you did not complete the transfer/registration by that cutoff, you will not be able to vote from abroad in 2026. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/comunicacao/noticias/2026/Marco/eleicoes-2026-confira-as-principais-datas-do-calendario-eleitoral?utm_source=openai))

Voting logistics and documents
- Where you vote: voting in the exterior takes place in sections organized at embassies, consulates or special sections authorized by the TSE; a minimum number of registered electors is required for a section to be installed (rules on aggregation exist). ([mpce.mp.br](https://www.mpce.mp.br/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/RESOLUCAO-No-23.669-DE-14-DE-DEZEMBRO-DE-2021.-%E2%80%94-Tribunal-Superior-Eleitoral.pdf))  
- What to take: bring an official Brazilian photo ID (or passport with filiation as required by the Zona ZZ instructions) and any documentation the consulate asks for; the TSE/TRE guidance lists the documents and procedures. ([tre-df.jus.br](https://www.tre-df.jus.br/servicos-eleitorais/eleitor-no-exterior/eleitor-no-exterior?utm_source=openai))

Obligation to vote / consequences
- By law voting is compulsory for literate Brazilians aged 18–70 (this legal rule also applies to electors resident abroad). TRE/TSE guidance flags the same legal obligation, although practical enforcement of fines for non‑voting abroad is uneven; nevertheless, keeping your registration regular avoids problems (for example with certain public services or passport/CPF checks). ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/servicos-eleitorais/servicos/ouvidoria-content/perguntas-frequentes-faq-2023?utm_source=openai))

If you missed the deadline
- If you missed the 6 May 2026 deadline to transfer/register in the Zona ZZ, your main options are: (a) if your voter registration is still in a Brazilian municipality, return to Brazil and vote there (or use the justificativa procedure if you are abroad and do not vote), or (b) regularize your registration after the election so you can vote in the next pleito. The TSE explains the posts‑election justification and regularization processes. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/servicos-eleitorais/eleitorado-no-exterior?utm_source=openai))

If you want, I can
- Check and walk you through the exact steps to (a) see whether you already have your registration linked to the Zona ZZ (I can show where on the TSE site to check), or (b) show step‑by‑step how to use the Título Net Exterior and what documents to prepare for the consulate. Which would you prefer?

answer_text:

Because Brazil uses a single (unified) election schedule referenced to Brasília time, the local clock times differ by time zone. Common conversions are:  
- UTC−02:00 (Fernando de Noronha): 09:00–18:00 local.  
- UTC−03:00 (Brasília, São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, most of the country): 08:00–17:00 local.  
- UTC−04:00 (parts of the North / West: e.g., Manaus, Porto Velho, Cuiabá, Campo Grande): 07:00–16:00 local.  
- UTC−05:00 (Acre): 06:00–15:00 local. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Brazilian_general_election?utm_source=openai))

prompt: I want to vote in person in the Brazilian general election 2026 (Brazil). What ID or documents do I need to bring with me to be allowed to vote?
answer_text:
Short answer: on election day you must present either (A) one official photo ID, or (B) the e‑Título app showing your photo (only if your photo/biometrics are already registered). Bring at least one of the accepted documents below to be allowed to vote. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/comunicacao/noticias/2026/Abril/eleitor-em-dia-saiba-quais-documentos-sao-validos-para-votar-nas-eleicoes-2026?utm_source=openai))

Which documents are accepted
- e‑Título (digital voter card) with your photograph — only valid for voters whose photo/biometrics are registered and visible in the app. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/comunicacao/noticias/2024/Marco/use-o-e-titulo-com-foto-para-confirmar-sua-identidade-e-votar-nas-eleicoes-2024?utm_source=openai))  
- National identity (RG) or “identidade social.” ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/eleicoes-2026-content/normas-e-documentacoes/arquivos-2026/resolucao-e-voto-atos-gerais/%40%40download/file/Resolucao-e-voto-atos-gerais.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
- Passport. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/eleicoes-2026-content/normas-e-documentacoes/arquivos-2026/resolucao-e-voto-atos-gerais/%40%40download/file/Resolucao-e-voto-atos-gerais.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
- National driver’s licence (CNH). ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/eleicoes-2026-content/normas-e-documentacoes/arquivos-2026/resolucao-e-voto-atos-gerais/%40%40download/file/Resolucao-e-voto-atos-gerais.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
- Military reservist certificate (Certificado de Reservista). ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/eleicoes-2026-content/normas-e-documentacoes/arquivos-2026/resolucao-e-voto-atos-gerais/%40%40download/file/Resolucao-e-voto-atos-gerais.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
- Work card in physical (printed) form (Carteira de Trabalho — physical paper/card form). Note: the Carteira de Trabalho digital (app) is not accepted as ID at the polling place. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/eleicoes-2026-content/normas-e-documentacoes/arquivos-2026/resolucao-e-voto-atos-gerais/%40%40download/file/Resolucao-e-voto-atos-gerais.pdf?utm_source=openai))  
- Professional ID cards recognized by law (for example OAB card), when they include a photo. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/eleicoes-2026-content/normas-e-documentacoes/arquivos-2026/resolucao-e-voto-atos-gerais/%40%40download/file/Resolucao-e-voto-atos-gerais.pdf?utm_source=openai))

Other important points
- You do NOT need to carry the printed voter registration (Título de Eleitor) to vote; the requirement is an official photo ID or the e‑Título with photo. If you bring the physical Título it does not replace the need for an ID with photo. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/comunicacao/noticias/2024/Marco/preciso-levar-o-titulo-para-votar-veja-quais-sao-os-documentos-aceitos-no-dia-da-eleicao?utm_source=openai))  
- Documents may be accepted even if their expiration date has passed, provided the identity can be confirmed. The polling official may request additional checks if identity is doubtful. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/legislacao/compilada/res/2024/resolucao-no-23-736-de-27-de-fevereiro-de-2024?utm_source=openai))

Practical tips
- If you plan to use the e‑Título, update the app and confirm your photo is showing well before election day. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/comunicacao/noticias/2024/Marco/use-o-e-titulo-com-foto-para-confirmar-sua-identidade-e-votar-nas-eleicoes-2024?utm_source=openai))  
- Bring at least one government photo ID (RG, CNH or passport) as a reliable backup.  
- Check the TSE or your Regional Electoral Court (TRE) website shortly before voting in case of procedural updates. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/eleicoes-2026-content/normas-e-documentacoes/arquivos-2026/resolucao-e-voto-atos-gerais/%40%40download/file/Resolucao-e-voto-atos-gerais.pdf?utm_source=openai))

Dates (so you don’t rely on “today/tomorrow”): the first round of the 2026 general election is scheduled for October 4, 2026; if needed, the second round is scheduled for October 25, 2026. ([tse.jus.br](https://www.tse.jus.br/comunicacao/noticias/2026/Abril/eleitor-em-dia-saiba-quais-documentos-sao-validos-para-votar-nas-eleicoes-2026?utm_source=openai))

If you want, tell me (1) whether you’re already registered to vote in Brazil and (2) whether you’ll be voting in the municipality where you’re registered — I can then check any local guidance (e.g., biometric requirements) for your specific case.

prompt: I have a postal/mail ballot for the Saxony-Anhalt state election (Landtagswahl) 2026 in Saxony-Anhalt, Germany. Does my ballot need to physically ARRIVE by election day, or is it enough that it is postmarked by election day? What exactly is the deadline?
answer_text:
Short answer: Your postal (Brief-)vote must actually arrive at the responsible election office by the end of voting on election day — a postmark alone is not enough.

Details and exact deadline
- Election day for the Saxony‑Anhalt Landtag is Sunday, 6 September 2026; polling places (and the deadline for receiving postal votes) end at 18:00 (6:00 p.m.). ([wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de](https://wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de/zu-den-wahlen/landtagswahl/faqs-zur-landtagswahl-2026))  
- The Saxony‑Anhalt Wahlgesetz requires that the red Wahlbrief "so rechtzeitig zu übersenden [ist], dass der Wahlbrief spätestens am Wahltage bis 18 Uhr eingeht" (i.e. it must be received by the Kreiswahlleiter by 18:00 on election day). A mere postmark does not satisfy that receipt requirement. ([landtag.sachsen-anhalt.de](https://www.landtag.sachsen-anhalt.de/fileadmin/Downloads/Rechtsgrundlagen/Gesetze_8.WP/2021_Wahlgesetz_LWG.pdf))  
- Local election authorities must accept/deliver and collect Wahlbriefe up to the end of the voting period on election day; you may also hand the envelope in personally at the address printed on the envelope until 18:00. ([wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de](https://wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de/fileadmin/Bibliothek/Politik_und_Verwaltung/MI/wahlen/PDF/2026-04-01_Durchfuehrungserlass_Landtagswahl.pdf))

What you can do if you’re worried
- If you’ve already mailed it and fear it may arrive late, try to hand‑deliver the sealed Wahlbrief to your municipal election office (or the address printed on the envelope) before 18:00 on 6 Sep 2026. ([bundeswahlleiterin.de](https://www.bundeswahlleiterin.de/bundestagswahlen/2025/informationen-waehler/briefwahl.html))  
- If you tell me your town/municipality, I can look up the exact contact/address of your Kreis‑/Gemeindewahlleiter so you can call or (if possible) deliver the envelope in person.

Sources: Saxony‑Anhalt Landeswahlgesetz §28 (briefwahl rule). Landeswahlleiter FAQs (election date/time). Durchführungs‑Erlass zur Landtagswahl (handling/receipt rules). Bundeswahlleiter guidance on Briefwahl. ([landtag.sachsen-anhalt.de](https://www.landtag.sachsen-anhalt.de/fileadmin/Downloads/Rechtsgrundlagen/Gesetze_8.WP/2021_Wahlgesetz_LWG.pdf))

prompt: I want to vote in the Saxony-Anhalt state election (Landtagswahl) 2026 on 6 September 2026 in Saxony-Anhalt, Germany. Do I need to register to vote, and if so, what is the deadline?
answer_text:
Short answer: No separate “voter registration” is required. If you are registered as a resident (Hauptwohnung) in Saxony‑Anhalt and meet the legal eligibility rules, the municipality will enter you automatically in the electoral roll. ([wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de](https://wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de/zu-den-wahlen/allgemeine-informationen-zur-landtagswahl/wer-kann-waehlen-oder-gewaehlt-werden?cHash=81ffe5e5aeac75e45c189ad6b31f86de&tx_news_pi1%5BcurrentPage%5D=0))

What matters and important deadlines
- Eligibility: you must be German (Art. 116 GG), be 18 or older on election day (6 Sept 2026) and have had your residence in Saxony‑Anhalt for at least three months before the vote. That means your date of first residence must be on or before 6 June 2026 (the day of moving in is counted). ([wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de](https://wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de/zu-den-wahlen/allgemeine-informationen-zur-landtagswahl/wer-kann-waehlen-oder-gewaehlt-werden?cHash=81ffe5e5aeac75e45c189ad6b31f86de&tx_news_pi1%5BcurrentPage%5D=0))  
- If you want to vote by post (Briefwahl): the local authorities accept Wahlschein‑Anträge up to 15:00 on 5 Sept 2026 (the day before the election). Postal ballots must arrive by the time the polls close on election day (18:00). Check your municipality for practical postal timing. ([wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de](https://wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de/fileadmin/Bibliothek/Politik_und_Verwaltung/MI/wahlen/PDF/2026_Terminkalender_fuer_die_Vorbereitung_und_Durchfuehrung_der_Landtagswahl.pdf))
- You will normally receive a Wahlbenachrichtigung (polling notice) a few weeks before the election; if you don’t get one, contact your local Einwohnermeldeamt / Wahlamt to check whether you’re in the Wählerverzeichnis. ([bundeswahlleiterin.de](https://www.bundeswahlleiterin.de/service/glossar/w/wahlbenachrichtigung.html?utm_source=openai))

If any of the following apply, tell me and I’ll give specific steps:
- You recently moved to Saxony‑Anhalt (after 6 June 2026).  
- You are a German living abroad who wants to vote.  
- You want exact contact details for your municipality’s Wahlamt so you can confirm your entry or request Briefwahl.

If you already requested postal voting (Wahlschein), bring the Wahlschein plus an ID. These are the usual and accepted documents; the Wahlvorstand may ask for ID if the notification is missing or if there are doubts about your identity. ([wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de](https://wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de/fileadmin/Bibliothek/Politik_und_Verwaltung/MI/wahlen/PDF/2026-04-01_Durchfuehrungserlass_Landtagswahl.pdf))

Details / practical checklist
- Wahlbenachrichtigung (Wahlbenachrichtigungskarte): normally sufficient to be admitted to vote. ([wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de](https://wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de/fileadmin/Bibliothek/Politik_und_Verwaltung/MI/wahlen/PDF/2026-04-01_Durchfuehrungserlass_Landtagswahl.pdf))  
- Official photo ID (if asked or if you forgot the notification): Personalausweis, Reisepass or another amtliches Lichtbilddokument such as a Führerschein. The Wahlvorstand can require you to identify yourself and compare your face with the photo. ([wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de](https://wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de/fileadmin/Bibliothek/Politik_und_Verwaltung/MI/wahlen/PDF/2026-04-01_Durchfuehrungserlass_Landtagswahl.pdf))  
- Wahlschein (postal‑vote certificate): if you have obtained a Wahlschein/briefwahl, you must present it and hand it over to the Wahlvorsteher when voting in person. ([wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de](https://wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de/fileadmin/Bibliothek/Politik_und_Verwaltung/MI/wahlen/PDF/2026-04-01_Durchfuehrungserlass_Landtagswahl.pdf))

Extra tips
- You do not legally have to carry the notification card in every case, but bringing both the card and an ID makes the process fastest and avoids delays. ([wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de](https://wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de/fileadmin/Bibliothek/Politik_und_Verwaltung/MI/wahlen/PDF/2026-04-01_Durchfuehrungserlass_Landtagswahl.pdf))  
- If you have any doubt (e.g., you moved and aren’t sure you’re in the voters’ register, or you lost both card and ID), contact your local Wahlbüro/Wahlamt before election day — contact details are on your municipality’s website or the Landeswahlleiter site. ([wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de](https://wahlen.sachsen-anhalt.de/zu-den-wahlen/landtagswahl/faqs-zur-landtagswahl-2026))

If you want, tell me your town/municipality and I can look up the local Wahlamt phone/address for you.

prompt: I will be abroad during the Finnish parliamentary election (Eduskunta) 2027 (18 April 2027). Can I vote in advance from abroad, and on exactly which dates is advance voting open for voters outside the country?
answer_text:
Short answer: Yes. For the 2027 Eduskunta (parliamentary) election (election day Sunday, 18 April 2027) advance voting abroad will be April 7–10, 2027 (advance voting in Finland is April 7–13, 2027). ([finlex.fi](https://www.finlex.fi/fi/lainsaadanto/1998/714?utm_source=openai))

What that means for you
- If you are abroad during that period you can vote in person at designated Finnish diplomatic missions (embassies/consulates) on the advance‑voting days 7–10 April 2027. ([um.fi](https://um.fi/fa/aanestaminen-ulkomailla?utm_source=openai))  
- If you will be abroad for the entire advance period and on election day you may instead vote by post (kirjeäänestys). You must order the postal voting package from the Ministry of Justice’s ordering service well in advance. ([um.fi](https://um.fi/fa/aanestaminen-ulkomailla?utm_source=openai))
- Always bring a valid photographic ID (passport, Finnish identity card or driving licence) when voting in person. ([stadissa.fi](https://www.stadissa.fi/tapahtumat/91787/kuntavaalit-2025-ennakkoaanestys-ohjeet-ja-paikat?utm_source=openai))

Why those dates
- The Election Act sets the advance‑voting window to begin on the 11th day before election day and to end abroad on the 8th day before election day (and in Finland on the 5th day before election day). For 18 April 2027 that gives 7 April as the start and 10 April as the end for voting abroad. ([finlex.fi](https://www.finlex.fi/fi/lainsaadanto/1998/714?utm_source=openai))

Practical tips
- Check the nearest Finnish mission’s website (they publish exact opening times and whether they are providing advance voting), and check vaalit.fi for the official polling‑place list and the postal‑voting ordering link (vaalit.fi/kirjeaanestys). ([um.fi](https://um.fi/fa/aanestaminen-ulkomailla?utm_source=openai))  
- Make sure your contact/address details in the Finnish population register (DVV / Digi‑ ja väestötietovirasto) are up to date so you receive any election mailings. ([um.fi](https://um.fi/fa/aanestaminen-ulkomailla?utm_source=openai))

If you want, tell me whether you plan to vote in person at an embassy or by post and which country you’ll be in — I can look up the nearest Finnish mission and link you to its advance‑voting info.

prompt: Am I eligible to vote in the Finnish parliamentary election (Eduskunta) 2027 (Finland)? What are the citizenship, age, and residency requirements?
answer_text:
Short answer: You can vote in Finland’s parliamentary (Eduskunta) election 2027 only if you are a Finnish citizen and you are 18 years old on (or before) the election day. Your place of residence does not stop a Finnish citizen from voting — Finnish citizens living abroad may also vote (usually by post or at a Finnish mission). Non‑Finnish citizens (including other EU citizens) do not have the right to vote in parliamentary elections. ([suomi.fi](https://www.suomi.fi/citizen/rights-and-obligations/fundamental-rights-and-civic-activity/guide/voting/voting-in-parliamentary-elections?utm_source=openai))

Details and how to check

- Citizenship: Parliamentary elections are reserved to Finnish citizens. (Other elections — municipal or European Parliament — have different rules for EU/EEA citizens.) ([stat.fi](https://stat.fi/en/documentation/documentation-of-statistics/evaa?utm_source=openai))  
- Age: You must have reached age 18 no later than on election day. (Many local sources list the 2027 parliamentary election day as Sunday 18 April 2027.) ([suomi.fi](https://www.suomi.fi/citizen/rights-and-obligations/fundamental-rights-and-civic-activity/guide/voting?utm_source=openai))  
- Residency: For Eduskunta elections the right to vote is based on citizenship, not on where you live — Finnish citizens resident abroad are entitled to vote and can normally vote by post or at diplomatic missions. If you live in Finland you will be included in the voting register via the Population Information System and will receive a polling/advance‑voting notice. ([suomi.fi](https://www.suomi.fi/citizen/rights-and-participation-in-society/welcome-to-adulthood/guide/right-to-influence-society/voting-in-parliamentary-elections?utm_source=openai))  
- Exceptions: the law excludes persons who have lost voting rights for legally specified reasons (for example certain legal incapacity situations); see the official election guidance for details. ([stat.fi](https://stat.fi/en/documentation/documentation-of-statistics/evaa?utm_source=openai))

What to do next (quick checklist)
1. If you are a Finnish citizen and were born on or before the election day (e.g., born on or before 18 Apr 2009 for the 18 Apr 2027 date), you are eligible. ([suomi.fi](https://www.suomi.fi/citizen/rights-and-obligations/fundamental-rights-and-civic-activity/guide/voting?utm_source=openai))  
2. If you live in Finland: you will normally receive a polling card (or a Suomi.fi message). If you don’t receive anything, check with your municipality or the Digital and Population Data Services Agency (Population Information System). ([suomi.fi](https://www.suomi.fi/citizen/rights-and-participation-in-society/welcome-to-adulthood/guide/right-to-influence-society/voting-in-parliamentary-elections?utm_source=openai))  
3. If you live abroad: contact the nearest Finnish mission (embassy/consulate) or follow the instructions on the official election pages to vote by post or at the mission. ([suomi.fi](https://www.suomi.fi/citizen/rights-and-participation-in-society/welcome-to-adulthood/guide/right-to-influence-society/voting-in-parliamentary-elections?utm_source=openai))

If you want, tell me (1) whether you are a Finnish citizen, (2) your date of birth, and (3) whether you live in Finland or abroad — I can confirm plain‑language eligibility for you and point to the exact official contact/registration page you should use.

prompt: I'm going to vote in person in the Finnish parliamentary election (Eduskunta) 2027 on 18 April 2027. What time do polling stations open and close on election day in Finland?
answer_text:
On election day in Finland polling stations are open from 9:00 to 20:00 (9 a.m.–8 p.m.) local time. ([valtioneuvosto.fi](https://valtioneuvosto.fi/en/-/1410853/election-day-for-county-and-municipal-elections-is-sunday-13-april-2025?utm_source=openai))

The 2027 parliamentary (Eduskunta) election is scheduled for Sunday 18 April 2027, so those hours apply on that date — i.e. 9:00–20:00 Finland local time. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2027_Finnish_parliamentary_election?utm_source=openai))

Because Finland will be observing summer time then, 18 April 2027 is Eastern European Summer Time (EEST), which is UTC+3 — so the polling-station hours are 09:00–20:00 EEST (UTC+3). ([timeanddate.com](https://www.timeanddate.com/time/change/%40658226?year=2027&utm_source=openai))

If you want, I can also look up advance-voting dates, what ID to bring, or your local polling station address.
